source: BPMN 2.0 best practices plus project-specific modeling conventions

instructions.title: Model one process outcome clearly
instructions.guideline: Use one clear start and explicit end states so the happy path and exception paths are understandable.
instructions.rationale: Clear process boundaries improve readability and prevent hidden dead-ends.

instructions.title: Start and end events are mandatory
instructions.guideline: Always model at least one start event and one end event.
instructions.rationale: Events define lifecycle boundaries and support BPMN-compliant execution semantics.

instructions.title: Use action-oriented task labels
instructions.guideline: Task labels should be verb-first and short, e.g. 'Bestellung prüfen'.
instructions.rationale: Verb-based labels reduce ambiguity and improve process comprehension.

instructions.title: Use gateways only for real decisions
instructions.guideline: Create XOR gateways only when process flow can branch based on conditions.
instructions.rationale: Unnecessary gateways add noise and reduce model quality.

instructions.title: Label gateway branches consistently
instructions.guideline: Use explicit condition labels, preferably 'Ja' and 'Nein' for binary decisions.
instructions.rationale: Consistent branch labels reduce interpretation errors.

instructions.title: Set a default path for XOR gateways
instructions.guideline: Define a default outgoing sequence flow when one path should be the fallback.
instructions.rationale: Default flows improve deterministic behavior and readability.

instructions.title: Assign every task to a responsible role
instructions.guideline: Each activity must belong to exactly one lane/role.
instructions.rationale: Clear responsibilities are required for operational clarity.

instructions.title: Keep flow direction left-to-right
instructions.guideline: Use dominant forward edges and avoid back edges unless loops are required.

instructions.title: Avoid edge crossings in lane area
instructions.guideline: Route sequence flows orthogonally and avoid non-endpoint crossings.
instructions.rationale: Crossings lower readability and hide control-flow intent.

instructions.title: No dead-end activities
instructions.guideline: Every non-end step must lead to another step or branch.
instructions.rationale: Dead ends indicate incomplete process logic.

instructions.title: Use short and unique labels
instructions.guideline: Prefer concise labels (up to 3 words) and avoid duplicate tasks with identical role and meaning.
instructions.rationale: Compact unique naming keeps the model compact and easier to parse.

instructions.title: Boundary timers need explicit target
instructions.guideline: Each boundary timer must define where the flow continues.
instructions.rationale: Timer semantics are invalid without continuation target.

antiPatterns.name: Gateway with one outgoing path
antiPatterns.whyItIsBad: Adds visual complexity without decision semantics.
antiPatterns.fix: Replace with direct sequence flow from predecessor to successor.

antiPatterns.name: Unlabeled gateway branches
antiPatterns.fix: Add branch conditions with explicit wording, ideally Ja/Nein.

antiPatterns.name: Cross-lane activity ownership ambiguity
antiPatterns.fix: Ensure each step has one role and is rendered in the matching lane.

antiPatterns.name: Excessive back loops
antiPatterns.whyItIsBad: Reduces readability and creates routing clutter.
antiPatterns.fix: Use explicit correction loops and isolate return paths.

qualityChecklist: At least one start event and one end event exist.
Each non-end node has outgoing flow.
Each gateway has two or more meaningful branches.
Branch labels are explicit and normalized.
No non-endpoint line crossings in lane area.
Main path stays near lane center for readability.
